victim_side: Lebanese and Palestinian civilians in West Beirut
excerpt: TIME described the encircled western half of Beirut as holding roughly half a million residents during the siege.
confidence_note: Contemporaneous journalistic estimate, not a census.

victim_side: PLO armed personnel in West Beirut
excerpt: TIME reported planning for the withdrawal of about six thousand PLO guerrillas from West Beirut.
Adelman categorized 30,000 to 60,000 West Beirut citizens as having fled the city temporarily during the fighting.
confidence_note: Useful for lowering the civilian denominator from the half-million baseline.

victim_side: PLO armed personnel in West Beirut
excerpt: Sayigh estimated no more than about two thousand PLO regulars in Beirut plus around four to five thousand part-time fighters, excluding roughly one thousand Syrian soldiers and three thousand PLA under Syrian control.
transform_note: Claim values sum the regular and part-time fighter counts given by the source.
confidence_note: Strong for PLO presence, but exclusion of PLA/Syrian personnel matters for upper military-denominator scenarios.

excerpt: MERIP reported that the official police record for those killed in Beirut since 13 June stood at 3,934 as of 10 August.
confidence_note: Important floor inside the episode, but it stops before the last eleven days of the siege.

excerpt: Washington Post's summary of the An Nahar survey reported 5,515 killed in Beirut and its suburbs, without a civilian-military split.
confidence_note: Useful as a mixed-status lower anchor for the late-Beirut total, but slightly broader than the West Beirut core siege footprint.

excerpt: The Christian Science Monitor reported a Lebanese police total of 6,775 deaths in Beirut up to the August cease-fire.
confidence_note: Episode date overlap is imperfect because the article summarizes figures up to the August cease-fire.

excerpt: The same police-based report stated that about 84 percent of Beirut's dead were civilians.
confidence_note: Central status-split input, but it must be transferred carefully to mixed-status Beirut totals.

excerpt: Adelman reported roughly 107,000 registered Palestinian refugees in southern Lebanon, with 60,000 in camps and 47,000 in towns and cities.
confidence_note: Useful denominator anchor for refugee populations, not for all civilians in the south.

victim_side: Palestinian civilians in southern camp belt
excerpt: Adelman's south-camp figures imply a camp-belt civilian population somewhere between sixty thousand registered refugees alone and roughly eighty-five thousand if unregistered camp refugees are added.
transform_note: Best value is the midpoint of the registered-only and registered-plus-unregistered camp counts.
confidence_note: Good for camp-specific sub-footprints; still narrower than all civilians in the south.

excerpt: Adelman added that another thirty thousand people in southern Lebanon probably fled temporarily to avoid the fighting.
confidence_note: Useful for denominator sensitivity but not enough to reconstruct a full south-wide civilian exposure count.

victim_side: PLO armed personnel in southern Lebanon
excerpt: Sayigh's south-Lebanon deployment estimate implies roughly four thousand PLO and allied local-support fighters in the south, including the Sidon region.
transform_note: Best value sums the source's 2,000 full-time and 2,000 allied/local-support figures.
confidence_note: Good belligerent-denominator anchor for the south.

victim_side: Lebanese and Palestinian civilians in southern Lebanon
excerpt: Washington Post's summary of the An Nahar survey reported 1,709 civilians killed in southern Lebanon.
confidence_note: The cleanest south-wide civilian death claim found, but still based on a press summary of the underlying survey.

victim_side: PLO, Syrian, and Lebanese militia armed personnel in southern Lebanon
excerpt: The same An Nahar summary reported 5,862 troops from the PLO, Syrian Army, and Lebanese militias killed in southern Lebanon.
confidence_note: This high figure sits far above some PLO-specific scholarly estimates, so it should be treated as an upper-structure mixed-combatant count rather than a settled truth.

victim_side: Civilians in Tyre, Sidon, and Nabatiyeh districts
geographic_scope: Tyre district, Sidon district, Nabatiyeh district
excerpt: Mandel's reproduction of IDF-spokesman figures implies 331 civilian deaths across Tyre, Sidon, and Nabatiyeh districts.
transform_note: Best value sums 56 + 265 + 10 from the district figures quoted by the source.
confidence_note: Useful low-official bound only; almost certainly incomplete relative to Lebanese and humanitarian reporting.

excerpt: MERIP relayed a doctor's estimate that at least 1,500 civilians died in Ayn al-Hilwa alone, illustrating the size of the gap between low official and high humanitarian narratives.
confidence_note: Single-site claim, not a south-wide total; best used to document disagreement.

victim_side: Palestinian civilians in Ayn al-Hilwa and Rashidiyya
excerpt: MERIP gave a combined population of roughly eighty thousand for Ayn al-Hilwa and Rashidiyya.
confidence_note: Helpful for a narrow camp-footprint denominator, not for all civilians in the south.

victim_side: Palestinian refugees and Lebanese civilians in Sabra-Shatila
geographic_scope: Sabra and Shatila area, southwest Beirut
excerpt: EBSCO summarized Sabra and Shatila as an area of about three square kilometers with about ninety thousand inhabitants.
confidence_note: Tertiary and therefore too weak, on its own, for a final DR denominator.

victim_side: Palestinian refugees and Lebanese civilians in Sabra-Shatila
excerpt: EBSCO reports that the ICRC had counted 2,750 bodies by 23 September after the massacre in Sabra and Shatila.
transform_note: Count date is later than the event, but the deaths belong to the 16-18 September massacre episode.
confidence_note: Good massacre-scale indicator, but still tertiary and not enough to settle the final toll.
